LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT NOVA PARTNERS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE FOR CROSS-COMPLAINANTS TO ALLEGE FACTS ESTABLISHING THAT NOVA PARTNERS OWED A DUTY TO CROSS-COMPLAINANTS. THE DEMURRER TO THE REMAINING CAUSES OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. AS TO CROSS-COMPLAINANT TRAVELERS, THE CLAIM FOR BREACH OF PERFORMANCE BOND IN THE UNDERLYING COMPLAINT DOES NOT SUPPORT CAUSES OF ACTION FOR EQUITABLE RELIEF FROM CROSS-DEFENDANT. AS TO CROSS-COMPLAINANT GILBANE, THE COMPLAINT FAILS TO ALLEGE TORT CAUSES OF ACTION AGAINST IT THAT WOULD PERMIT GILBANE TO SEEK EQUITABLE RELIEF FROM CROSS-DEFENDANT NOVA. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312.) JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON, II; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: ANN DELGADO, CSR# 11185. (302/AJR)

Counsel represent to the Court that they have reached the following stipulation: 1.) that the settling parties shall enter into a good faith settlement for $3.75 million; 2.) Gilbane Building Company shall forfeit comparative indemnity claims, and 3.) the entire $3.75 million shall set off any ultimate judgment obtained against Gilbane Building Company in this case. The Court states it will not make a determination at this time regarding allocation on design, as that will be up for the trial department. The Court makes a disclosure regarding his friendship with general counsel for Regents, that he sees no reason why he cannot be fair and impartial, but, moreoever, that the issue is moot now that parties have reached a stipulation in this matter. The Court vacates its tentative ruling as to DEFENDANT GILBANE BUILDING COMPANY'S Motion To Contest Flad and Associates, Inc. and Affiliated Engineers, Inc.'s Application For Determination of Good Faith Settlement. Parties shall submit a stipulation and proposed order to the Court for signature. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-10-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Stephen K. Anderson, Esq. of Van De Poel, Levy & Allen, LLP, T:925.934.6102 ext.305, for cross defendant and moving party;
Daniel Olsen, Esq. of Clapp Moroney Bellagamba Vucinich Beeman & Scheley, T:925.734.0990, for defendants/cross complainants Gilbane and Travelers;
John R. Meehan, Esq. of Severson & Werson, APC, T:415.398.3344 x5560, for defendants Flad and AEI, by Court Call;
Amy Kuo Alexander, Esq. of Gordon & Rees, LLP, T:415.986.5900, for cross defendants Langan.

RULING: The matter was argued. After arguments, the Court adopted the tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT NOVA PARTNERS, INC.'s DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is sustained as to the negligence cause of action with ten days leave for Cross-Complainants to allege facts establishing that Nova Partners owed a duty to Cross-Complainants. The Demurrer to the remaining causes of action is sustained without leave to amend. As to Cross-Complainant Travelers, the claim for breach of performance bond in the underlying Complaint does not support causes of action for equitable relief from Cross-Defendant. As to Cross-Complainant Gilbane, the Complaint fails to allege tort causes of action against it that would permit Gilbane to seek equitable relief from Cross-Defendant Nova. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312.) Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR# 11185.
